# Substrate Systems

> Substrate Systems OÜ is a solo-founded Estonian software company building durable systems infrastructure. The company operates under the brand "Substrate" at substratesystems.io.

Substrate builds tools for long-horizon problems — software that prioritizes structural integrity, determinism, and longevity over velocity. The company's engineering philosophy: systems precede products, constraints enable clarity, foundations compound.

Founded by Hugo Ander Kivi. Registered in Estonia as Substrate Systems OÜ.

## Products

- [Endstate](https://substratesystems.io/endstate): Declarative Windows machine provisioning — captures installed apps and their settings, saves to a portable file, restores on a fresh Windows install. Uses winget. Lifetime license (€39). Engine is open source (Apache 2.0).
- [Endstate provisioning engine (GitHub)](https://github.com/Artexis10/endstate): Open-source Go CLI that handles app detection, installation, and settings backup/restore on Windows via winget. Apache 2.0 license.
- [Endstate GUI (GitHub)](https://github.com/Artexis10/endstate-gui): Desktop application built with Tauri (React + Rust). Open source (Apache 2.0), with a paid license for the pre-built installer.

## About

- [Why Endstate was built](https://substratesystems.io/endstate/why): The origin story — the problem of recreating a Windows environment from scratch, why existing tools (Ninite, winget alone, Chocolatey) don't solve the full capture-and-restore problem, and how Endstate addresses it.
- [Terms, Privacy & Refunds](https://substratesystems.io/terms): Lifetime non-transferable license (up to 3 machines), no telemetry or analytics, offline-first design, 30-day money-back guarantee. Contact: founder@substratesystems.io.

## Technical Details

- Endstate detects apps installed via traditional installers (EXE/MSI) and Microsoft Store
- 48 apps are sandbox-validated for automated installation
- Settings backup supported for: VS Code, Git, Neovim, Windows Terminal, PowerToys, Sublime Text, Windsurf, REAPER, Espanso, Flow Launcher, mpv, foobar2000
- Settings backup is always opt-in, never automatic
- Every settings restore creates a backup first — one-click revert
- App installs use standard Windows installers with UAC prompts — nothing installs silently
- License activation is a one-time online check; all other operations work offline
- Setup files are stored as plain files in Documents — no hidden databases, no cloud dependency
